population: Patients diagnosed with sepsis under the sepsis-3 criteria admitted to the ICU of Guangdong Provincial People’s Hospital.
predictor: Presepsin level, SOFA score, APACHE II score, PCT, and lactate
timing: Measured upon admission to the ICU
method: Prospective observational study using binary logistic regression and ROC curve analysis.
performance: Presepsin AUROC: 0.703 (95% CI: 0.614–0.793); Lactate AUROC: 0.711 (95% CI: 0.622–0.800); SOFA score AUROC: 0.793 (95% CI: 0.716–0.870); Combination of SOFA + Presepsin AUROC: 0.817 (95% CI: 0.742–0.892). Best cutoff for Presepsin: 2,623 pg/mL (Sensitivity 62.71%, Specificity 72.6%).
notes: Evidence extracted from text, Table 1 (baseline characteristics), Table 2 (logistic regression), Figure 1 (box plots), and Figure 2 (ROC curves). The study concludes that presepsin is an independent risk factor for in-hospital mortality and its combination with SOFA score improves prognostic accuracy.
